title: Alternate Nostril Breathing
lead: Alternate nostril breathing, or Nadi Shodhana, is a yogic technique of breathing through one nostril at a time to promote balance and calm.

blocks.text: Alternate nostril breathing (Nadi Shodhana) involves gently closing one nostril at a time, alternating the breath between them.
It's a traditional yogic practice used to calm and 'balance'. Some studies suggest benefits for stress and attention; evidence is emerging.

items: Sit comfortably. Rest your left hand in your lap.
With your right thumb, gently close your right nostril; inhale through the left.
Close the left nostril with your ring finger; release the right and exhale through it.
Inhale through the right; then close it and exhale through the left.
That's one round. Continue smoothly for several rounds.

items.label: Best for
items.value: Calm, focus, a gentle centering ritual

blocks.text: Forcing the breath. Keep it slow, quiet, and effortless; stop if you feel dizzy.

resources.title: Insight Timer
